title: The two lines everybody writes
checkLabels.t4: The tab title must still be there
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed, or closed in the wrong order.
why.no-doctype: There is no DOCTYPE. Without it the browser switches into a mode for ancient sites and some things start behaving strangely.
why.no-meta: There is no meta with an encoding. Non-English letters will come out as question marks or gibberish on someone else's computer.
why.charset-not-in-head: The encoding is there, but not in head. It has to be the FIRST thing — the browser reads it before it reaches your text. Put it in body and it is already too late.
why.lost-title: The tab title is gone. You are adding two lines, not deleting old ones.
hint2.no-doctype: DOCTYPE is the first line of the file. Before <html>, not inside it.
hint3.no-doctype: The exact line is <!DOCTYPE html>. See the example.
hint2.no-meta: The tag is meta. It has no closing tag — it is a void element.
hint3.no-meta: It goes inside <head>, before <title>. See the example.
hint2.charset-not-in-head: Look at where you put it. Is it between <head> and </head>?
hint3.charset-not-in-head: The browser reads the head before it reaches the body. If the encoding is down there, it has already read your text wrong.
blocks.0.text: Open any website. Right click, "View page source". The first lines are the same everywhere.
blocks.1.text: They are not a formality. Each of them solves a real problem.
blocks.3.text: Two lines. You write them once and never think about them again. But it is worth knowing what they do.
blocks.5.text: Add them to your page. Run. You will not see a difference — that is exactly the point.
blocks.7.text: It tells the browser: this is modern HTML. Without it the browser switches into a mode for ancient nineties sites and some things start behaving strangely.
blocks.9.text: A computer stores letters as numbers. utf-8 is the table it uses to turn them back. Without it, every letter outside the English alphabet is a gamble.
blocks.10.text: "Try removing the meta and writing something in another alphabet. Then open it on someone else's computer. You will see question marks, gibberish or empty boxes. Your code works — it just lies."
